key_insight: A recurrent neural network can act as a fast surrogate for laminated ferromagnetic core behavior, capturing both hysteresis and eddy currents inside finite-element simulations.
engineering_relevance: This enables much more realistic electromagnetic machine design studies than saturation-only models, while keeping the added cost close to an anhysteretic simulation.
method: Trained an RNN surrogate for an isotropic laminated-core material model.
Embedded the surrogate in 2D magnetodynamic finite element simulations using a magnetic vector potential formulation.
Used artificially generated magnetic-field sequences to cover operating conditions typical of electrical machines.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports excellent agreement with the reference laminated-core model and a computational cost of about twice that of an anhysteretic simulation.
watch_item: Generalization depends on the diversity of the synthetic training sequences, so performance may degrade if deployed outside the trained magnetic-field regimes.
paper_title: Accounting for Hysteresis and Eddy Currents in Finite Element Simulations of Ferromagnetic Laminated Cores using a Recurrent Neural Network
